title: D001: Implement FluxCD infrastructure manifests
description:
**Feature ID:** CC-0008
**Size:** 🔨 medium
**Category:** infrastructure
**Priority:** high

---

## Open Questions

All three original questions have been answered by the user:

- **Q1 (Memcached Operator Helm chart)** → OCI registry at GHCR: `oci://ghcr.io/c5c3/charts` via a `c5c3-charts` HelmRepository (confirmed by `architecture/docs/09-implementation/07-ci-cd-and-packaging.md` lines 305–354)
- **Q2 (Default ClusterIssuer)** → Self-signed ClusterIssuer
- **Q3 (Kustomization includes infrastructure/)** → Yes, include `../infrastructure/`

**No remaining open questions.**

---

## Summary

Create the FluxCD infrastructure deployment manifests under `deploy/` for the CobaltCore development stack. This includes HelmRepository sources for five upstream registries (cert-manager, mariadb-operator, ESO, OpenBao, and the C5C3 OCI chart registry), HelmRelease definitions for four operators (cert-manager with a self-signed ClusterIssuer, MariaDB Operator, Memcached Operator, ESO) with production-ready values and dependency ordering, MariaDB Galera and Memcached instance CRs, and a Kustomize integration file. OpenBao's HelmRelease is excluded (CC-0009 scope).

## Scope

**Included:**
- 5 HelmRepository CRs in `deploy/flux-system/sources/` — `cert-manager` (`https://charts.jetstack.io`), `mariadb-operator` (`https://mariadb-operator.github.io/mariadb-operator`), `external-secrets` (`https://charts.external-secrets.io`), `openbao` (`https://openbao.github.io/openbao-helm`), `c5c3-charts` (`oci://ghcr.io/c5c3/charts`, type: `oci`)
- 4 HelmRelease CRs in `deploy/flux-system/releases/` — cert-manager, MariaDB Operator, Memcached Operator, ESO — with `dependsOn` chain and production values (`interval: 30m`, `install.crds: CreateReplace`, `upgrade.crds: CreateReplace`, `upgrade.remediation.retries: 3`)
- Self-signed ClusterIssuer bundled with cert-manager HelmRelease (post-install)
- MariaDB Galera CR (`deploy/infrastructure/mariadb.yaml`) — `k8s.mariadb.com/v1alpha1`, 3 replicas, Galera enabled, MaxScale, metrics
- Memcached CR (`deploy/infrastructure/memcached.yaml`) — `cache.c5c3.io/v1alpha1`, 3 replicas, anti-affinity, PDB, monitoring
- `deploy/flux-system/kustomization.yaml` referencing `sources/`, `releases/`, and `../infrastructure/`

**Excluded:**
- OpenBao HelmRelease and bootstrap scripts (CC-0009)
- ESO ClusterSecretStore and ExternalSecret CRs (CC-0009)
- `make deploy-infra` / `make teardown-infra` targets (CC-0010)
- Chainsaw E2E tests for infrastructure health (CC-0010)
- Valkey and RabbitMQ operators (YAGNI for Keystone-first strategy)
- Keystone Operator HelmRelease (CC-0017)
- Production OpenBao PKI ClusterIssuer (CC-0009)

## Visualization

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    subgraph sources["deploy/flux-system/sources/"]
        S1["HelmRepo: cert-manager\njetstack.io"]
        S2["HelmRepo: mariadb-operator\nGitHub Pages"]
        S3["HelmRepo: external-secrets\nexternal-secrets.io"]
        S4["HelmRepo: openbao\nGitHub Pages"]
        S5["HelmRepo: c5c3-charts\noci://ghcr.io/c5c3/charts"]
    end

    subgraph releases["deploy/flux-system/releases/"]
        R1["HelmRelease: cert-manager\n+ self-signed ClusterIssuer"]
        R2["HelmRelease: mariadb-operator"]
        R3["HelmRelease: external-secrets"]
        R4["HelmRelease: memcached-operator"]
    end

    subgraph infra["deploy/infrastructure/"]
        I1["MariaDB Galera CR\n3 replicas, MaxScale"]
        I2["Memcached CR\n3 replicas, PDB"]
    end

    K["deploy/flux-system/kustomization.yaml"]

    S1 --> R1
    S2 --> R2
    S3 --> R3
    S5 --> R4

    R2 -->|"dependsOn"| R1
    R3 -->|"dependsOn"| R1
    R4 -->|"dependsOn"| R1

    R2 -.->|"operator manages"| I1
    R4 -.->|"operator manages"| I2

    K -->|"references"| sources
    K -->|"references"| releases
    K -->|"references"| infra
```

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    CM["cert-manager\n#40;base layer#41;"] --> MDB["MariaDB Operator"]
    CM --> ESO["External Secrets Operator"]
    CM --> MC["Memcached Operator"]
    MDB -.-> MDBCR["MariaDB Galera CR"]
    MC -.-> MCCR["Memcached CR"]
```

## Key Components

- **`deploy/flux-system/sources/cert-manager.yaml`**: HelmRepository — `https://charts.jetstack.io`, `interval: 1h`, API `source.toolkit.fluxcd.io/v1`
- **`deploy/flux-system/sources/mariadb-operator.yaml`**: HelmRepository — `https://mariadb-operator.github.io/mariadb-operator`, `interval: 1h`
- **`deploy/flux-system/sources/external-secrets.yaml`**: HelmRepository — `https://charts.external-secrets.io`, `interval: 1h`
- **`deploy/flux-system/sources/openbao.yaml`**: HelmRepository — `https://openbao.github.io/openbao-helm`, `interval: 1h`
- **`deploy/flux-system/sources/c5c3-charts.yaml`**: HelmRepository — `oci://ghcr.io/c5c3/charts`, `type: oci`, `interval: 1h` (serves memcached-operator chart)
- **`deploy/flux-system/releases/cert-manager.yaml`**: HelmRelease in `cert-manager` namespace, chart version `>=1.0.0`, no dependencies (base layer), includes post-install self-signed `ClusterIssuer`
- **`deploy/flux-system/releases/mariadb-operator.yaml`**: HelmRelease in `mariadb-system` namespace, chart version `>=0.30.0 <1.0.0`, `dependsOn: cert-manager`, metrics + webhook enabled
- **`deploy/flux-system/releases/external-secrets.yaml`**: HelmRelease in `external-secrets` namespace, chart version `>=0.9.0`, `dependsOn: cert-manager`
- **`deploy/flux-system/releases/memcached-operator.yaml`**: HelmRelease in `memcached-system` namespace, chart `memcached-operator` from `c5c3-charts` sourceRef, chart version `>=0.1.0 <1.0.0`, `dependsOn: cert-manager`
- **`deploy/infrastructure/mariadb.yaml`**: MariaDB CR (`openstack-db`) in `openstack` namespace — `k8s.mariadb.com/v1alpha1`, Galera 3-replica, MaxScale 2-replica, 100Gi `ceph-rbd`, metrics enabled
- **`deploy/infrastructure/memcached.yaml`**: Memcached CR (`openstack-memcached`) in `openstack` namespace — `cache.c5c3.io/v1alpha1`, 3 replicas, 1024MB, 4096 max connections, soft anti-affinity, PDB minAvailable 2, monitoring exporter
- **`deploy/flux-system/kustomization.yaml`**: Kustomize file referencing `sources/`, `releases/`, and `../infrastructure/` for single-command `kubectl apply -k deploy/flux-system/` deployment

### Corrections from codebase research

Two details refined from the user's draft:

1. **Memcached CRD API group**: `cache.c5c3.io/v1alpha1` (not `memcached.c5c3.io/v1alpha1`) — confirmed from the fake CRD at `internal/common/testutil/fake_crds/memcached-operator/memcached.yaml` and the envtest integration test at `internal/common/testutil/envtest/integration_test.go:48`.

2. **Memcached-operator HelmRepository**: Named `c5c3-charts` (not a separate `memcached-operator` repo) pointing to `oci://ghcr.io/c5c3/charts` with `type: oci` — this is the shared OCI registry for all C5C3-owned charts, per `architecture/docs/09-implementation/07-ci-cd-and-packaging.md` lines 305–354. The HelmRelease references this via `sourceRef.name: c5c3-charts`.

stories.title: Platform engineer creates HelmRepository sources for upstream chart registries
stories.want: to define HelmRepository CRs for all required upstream Helm chart registries (cert-manager, mariadb-operator, external-secrets, openbao, c5c3-charts) in deploy/flux-system/sources/
stories.so_that: FluxCD can pull charts from the correct registries and HelmRelease CRs can reference them
criteria: 5 HelmRepository YAML files exist under deploy/flux-system/sources/ with correct names: cert-manager.yaml, mariadb-operator.yaml, external-secrets.yaml, openbao.yaml, c5c3-charts.yaml
All HelmRepository CRs use apiVersion source.toolkit.fluxcd.io/v1 and kind HelmRepository
All HelmRepository CRs have namespace flux-system and interval 1h
c5c3-charts.yaml uses spec.type: oci and url: oci://ghcr.io/c5c3/charts
All files include SPDX Apache-2.0 license headers

stories.title: Platform engineer deploys cert-manager as the base infrastructure layer with a self-signed ClusterIssuer
stories.want: to deploy cert-manager via HelmRelease with a self-signed ClusterIssuer bundled in the same manifest
stories.so_that: TLS certificate management is available for all dependent operators and a default issuer exists for development environments
criteria: deploy/flux-system/releases/cert-manager.yaml contains a HelmRelease CR targeting the cert-manager namespace
HelmRelease references sourceRef name cert-manager (HelmRepository) in namespace flux-system
Chart version constraint is >=1.0.0
HelmRelease has no dependsOn (base layer)
Same file contains a self-signed ClusterIssuer CR (cert-manager.io/v1) separated by --- document separator
HelmRelease includes install.crds: CreateReplace, upgrade.crds: CreateReplace, upgrade.remediation.retries: 3

stories.title: Platform engineer deploys infrastructure operators with correct dependency ordering
stories.want: to deploy MariaDB Operator, External Secrets Operator, and Memcached Operator via HelmRelease CRs that depend on cert-manager
stories.so_that: operators are installed in the correct order and CRDs are available before instance CRs are applied
criteria: deploy/flux-system/releases/ contains mariadb-operator.yaml, external-secrets.yaml, memcached-operator.yaml
All three HelmRelease CRs have dependsOn referencing cert-manager in cert-manager namespace
MariaDB Operator targets mariadb-system namespace with chart version >=0.30.0 <1.0.0 and enables metrics + webhook
ESO targets external-secrets namespace with chart version >=0.9.0
Memcached Operator targets memcached-system namespace with chart version >=0.1.0 <1.0.0 and sourceRef c5c3-charts
All HelmReleases use interval 30m, install.crds CreateReplace, upgrade.crds CreateReplace, upgrade.remediation.retries 3

stories.title: Platform engineer provisions a MariaDB Galera cluster for OpenStack services
stories.want: to deploy a MariaDB Galera CR in the openstack namespace with HA configuration
stories.so_that: OpenStack services (starting with Keystone) have a production-ready database backend
criteria: deploy/infrastructure/mariadb.yaml contains a MariaDB CR with apiVersion k8s.mariadb.com/v1alpha1
CR name is openstack-db in namespace openstack
spec.replicas is 3 with spec.galera.enabled true
spec.maxScale.enabled is true with spec.maxScale.replicas 2
spec.storage.size is 100Gi with storageClassName ceph-rbd
spec.metrics.enabled is true

stories.title: Platform engineer provisions a Memcached cluster for OpenStack session caching
stories.want: to deploy a Memcached CR in the openstack namespace with HA, anti-affinity, and monitoring
stories.so_that: OpenStack services have a production-ready distributed cache with disruption protection
criteria: deploy/infrastructure/memcached.yaml contains a Memcached CR with apiVersion cache.c5c3.io/v1alpha1 (NOT memcached.c5c3.io)
CR name is openstack-memcached in namespace openstack
spec.replicas is 3 with spec.config.maxMemoryMB 1024 and spec.config.maxConnections 4096
spec.highAvailability.podAntiAffinity is soft and spec.highAvailability.podDisruptionBudget.minAvailable is 2
spec.monitoring.enabled is true with exporter image prom/memcached-exporter:latest
spec.resources.requests includes memory 1Gi and cpu 500m

stories.title: Platform engineer deploys all infrastructure manifests via single kustomize command
stories.want: to apply all FluxCD sources, releases, and infrastructure CRs with kubectl apply -k deploy/flux-system/
stories.so_that: the entire infrastructure stack can be deployed or updated with one command
criteria: deploy/flux-system/kustomization.yaml exists with apiVersion kustomize.config.k8s.io/v1beta1
Kustomization resources list includes all 5 source files, all 4 release files, and both infrastructure CR files
kustomize build deploy/flux-system/ succeeds without errors
All 12 manifest files (5 sources + 4 releases + 2 infrastructure CRs + 1 ClusterIssuer) are included in the build output

requirements.description: HelmRepository CRs SHALL use apiVersion source.toolkit.fluxcd.io/v1 with correct source URLs and intervals
requirements.rationale: FluxCD source controller requires correct API version and valid repository URLs to sync charts

scenarios.name: Standard HTTPS HelmRepository created correctly
scenarios.when: a HelmRepository CR for cert-manager is applied
scenarios.then: the CR specifies apiVersion source.toolkit.fluxcd.io/v1, namespace flux-system, interval 1h, and url https://charts.jetstack.io
and_then: FluxCD source controller can sync chart metadata from the repository

scenarios.name: OCI HelmRepository created correctly
scenarios.when: a HelmRepository CR for c5c3-charts is applied
scenarios.then: the CR specifies type: oci and url: oci://ghcr.io/c5c3/charts in addition to standard fields
and_then: FluxCD resolves OCI artifacts from GHCR

scenarios.name: HelmRepository with invalid URL rejected
scenarios.when: a HelmRepository CR has a malformed or missing URL
scenarios.then: kubectl apply --dry-run=client rejects the manifest with a validation error
and_then: no invalid resource is persisted to the cluster

requirements.description: HelmRelease CRs SHALL use apiVersion helm.toolkit.fluxcd.io/v2 with production-ready install/upgrade settings
requirements.rationale: Consistent production settings (CRD management, remediation retries, intervals) ensure reliable operator lifecycle management

scenarios.name: HelmRelease includes production settings
scenarios.when: any HelmRelease CR is examined
scenarios.then: it contains interval 30m, install.crds CreateReplace, upgrade.crds CreateReplace, upgrade.remediation.retries 3
and_then: CRDs are managed during install and upgrade, and failed upgrades are retried

scenarios.name: HelmRelease creates target namespace
scenarios.when: a HelmRelease targets a namespace that does not exist
scenarios.then: the CR includes install.createNamespace true
and_then: the namespace is created automatically during first install

scenarios.name: HelmRelease sourceRef matches existing HelmRepository
scenarios.when: a HelmRelease references a sourceRef
scenarios.then: the sourceRef.name matches a HelmRepository name defined in deploy/flux-system/sources/ and sourceRef.namespace is flux-system
and_then: FluxCD can resolve the chart from the correct repository

requirements.description: HelmRelease CRs SHALL define correct dependency ordering via dependsOn with cert-manager as base layer
requirements.rationale: cert-manager CRDs must be available before dependent operators that use Certificate resources can be installed

scenarios.name: cert-manager has no dependencies (base layer)
scenarios.when: the cert-manager HelmRelease is examined
scenarios.then: it has no dependsOn field
and_then: cert-manager is the first operator installed

scenarios.name: Dependent operators reference cert-manager
scenarios.when: mariadb-operator, external-secrets, or memcached-operator HelmRelease is examined
scenarios.then: each has dependsOn containing name: cert-manager, namespace: cert-manager
and_then: these operators are installed only after cert-manager is ready

scenarios.name: No circular dependencies exist
scenarios.when: the full dependency graph of all HelmReleases is analyzed
scenarios.then: the graph is a directed acyclic graph with cert-manager as the sole root
and_then: FluxCD can compute a valid installation order

requirements.description: The cert-manager HelmRelease SHALL include a self-signed ClusterIssuer for development environments
requirements.rationale: A default ClusterIssuer enables TLS certificate provisioning immediately after cert-manager deployment without external CA configuration

scenarios.name: Self-signed ClusterIssuer is bundled
scenarios.when: deploy/flux-system/releases/cert-manager.yaml is examined
scenarios.then: the file contains a ClusterIssuer CR with apiVersion cert-manager.io/v1 and spec.selfSigned: {} separated from the HelmRelease by a --- document separator
and_then: the ClusterIssuer is deployed alongside cert-manager

scenarios.name: ClusterIssuer has correct metadata
scenarios.when: the ClusterIssuer CR is examined
scenarios.then: it has name selfsigned-issuer (or selfsigned-cluster-issuer) and no namespace (cluster-scoped)
and_then: any namespace can reference this issuer

scenarios.name: ClusterIssuer is included in kustomize build output
scenarios.when: kustomize build deploy/flux-system/ is executed
scenarios.then: the output includes both the HelmRelease and the ClusterIssuer resources
and_then: kubectl apply -k deploys both resources

requirements.description: The MariaDB Galera CR SHALL define a 3-replica HA cluster with MaxScale proxy, ceph-rbd storage, and metrics
requirements.rationale: OpenStack services require a production-grade database with synchronous replication, automatic failover, and observability

scenarios.name: MariaDB CR has correct API version and identity
scenarios.when: deploy/infrastructure/mariadb.yaml is examined
scenarios.then: it contains a MariaDB CR with apiVersion k8s.mariadb.com/v1alpha1, name openstack-db, namespace openstack
and_then: the CR matches the MariaDB Operator's expected schema

scenarios.name: Galera replication is enabled with 3 replicas
scenarios.when: the MariaDB CR spec is examined
scenarios.then: spec.replicas is 3 and spec.galera.enabled is true
and_then: synchronous multi-master replication is active across 3 nodes

scenarios.name: MaxScale proxy, storage, and metrics are configured
scenarios.when: the MariaDB CR spec is examined
scenarios.then: spec.maxScale.enabled is true with replicas 2, spec.storage.size is 100Gi with storageClassName ceph-rbd, spec.metrics.enabled is true
and_then: load balancing, persistent storage, and Prometheus scraping are configured

requirements.description: The Memcached CR SHALL define a 3-replica HA cluster with soft anti-affinity, PDB, resource requests, and monitoring
requirements.rationale: OpenStack session caching requires resilient distributed cache with disruption protection and observability

scenarios.name: Memcached CR uses correct API group
scenarios.when: deploy/infrastructure/memcached.yaml is examined
scenarios.then: it contains a Memcached CR with apiVersion cache.c5c3.io/v1alpha1 (NOT memcached.c5c3.io), name openstack-memcached, namespace openstack
and_then: the CR matches the Memcached Operator's registered CRD group

scenarios.name: HA configuration with anti-affinity and PDB
scenarios.when: the Memcached CR spec is examined
scenarios.then: spec.replicas is 3, spec.highAvailability.podAntiAffinity is soft, spec.highAvailability.podDisruptionBudget.minAvailable is 2
and_then: pods are spread across nodes and at least 2 remain available during disruptions

scenarios.name: Resource requests, cache config, and monitoring are set
scenarios.when: the Memcached CR spec is examined
scenarios.then: spec.config.maxMemoryMB is 1024, spec.config.maxConnections is 4096, spec.resources.requests.memory is 1Gi and cpu 500m, spec.monitoring.enabled is true with exporter image
and_then: cache is sized for OpenStack workloads with Prometheus metrics exposed

requirements.description: The Kustomization file SHALL aggregate all manifests from sources/, releases/, and ../infrastructure/ for single-command deployment
requirements.rationale: A single kustomize entry point enables reproducible deployment of the entire infrastructure stack

scenarios.name: Kustomization lists all manifest files
scenarios.when: deploy/flux-system/kustomization.yaml is examined
scenarios.then: the resources list includes all 5 source files (sources/*.yaml), all 4 release files (releases/*.yaml), and both infrastructure files (../infrastructure/*.yaml)
and_then: no manifest is omitted from the deployment

scenarios.name: kustomize build succeeds
scenarios.when: kustomize build deploy/flux-system/ is executed
scenarios.then: the command exits with status 0 and outputs valid multi-document YAML containing all resources
and_then: the output can be piped to kubectl apply

scenarios.name: Adding a new manifest requires only one line change
scenarios.when: a new HelmRelease (e.g., OpenBao in CC-0009) needs to be added
scenarios.then: only the kustomization.yaml resources list needs a new entry
and_then: the manifest structure is extensible without restructuring

requirements.description: All manifest files SHALL include the SPDX Apache-2.0 license header matching the project convention
requirements.rationale: License compliance requires consistent SPDX headers on all source files per project convention

scenarios.name: YAML files have correct SPDX header
scenarios.when: any YAML file under deploy/ is examined
scenarios.then: the first 3 lines are: '# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright 2026 SAP SE or an SAP affiliate company', '#', '# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0'
and_then: REUSE compliance checks pass

scenarios.name: SPDX header precedes YAML content
scenarios.when: a YAML file is parsed
scenarios.then: the SPDX header lines are comments and do not affect YAML parsing
and_then: kubectl apply and kustomize build ignore the header comments

tasks.title: Create deploy/flux-system/sources/ with 5 HelmRepository CRs (CC-0008, REQ-001, REQ-008)
tasks.description: Create the directory deploy/flux-system/sources/ and 5 HelmRepository YAML files. Each file follows the same structure: apiVersion source.toolkit.fluxcd.io/v1, kind HelmRepository, metadata.name and metadata.namespace flux-system, spec.interval 1h, spec.url. Files: cert-manager.yaml (url: https://charts.jetstack.io), mariadb-operator.yaml (url: https://mariadb-operator.github.io/mariadb-operator), external-secrets.yaml (url: https://charts.external-secrets.io), openbao.yaml (url: https://openbao.github.io/openbao-helm), c5c3-charts.yaml (url: oci://ghcr.io/c5c3/charts, spec.type: oci). All files include SPDX header. Remove deploy/.gitkeep after creating real files. Pattern reference: architecture/docs/09-implementation/07-ci-cd-and-packaging.md lines 310-318 and architecture/docs/09-implementation/09-openbao-deployment.md lines 10-16.

tasks.title: Create cert-manager HelmRelease with self-signed ClusterIssuer (CC-0008, REQ-002, REQ-003, REQ-004, REQ-008)
tasks.description: Create deploy/flux-system/releases/cert-manager.yaml containing two YAML documents separated by ---. Document 1: HelmRelease with apiVersion helm.toolkit.fluxcd.io/v2, metadata.name cert-manager, metadata.namespace cert-manager, spec.interval 30m, chart.spec.chart cert-manager, chart.spec.version '>=1.0.0', chart.spec.sourceRef (kind HelmRepository, name cert-manager, namespace flux-system), install.crds CreateReplace, install.createNamespace true, upgrade.crds CreateReplace, upgrade.remediation.retries 3. No dependsOn (base layer). Document 2: ClusterIssuer with apiVersion cert-manager.io/v1, metadata.name selfsigned-cluster-issuer, spec.selfSigned {}. Include SPDX header. Pattern reference: architecture/docs/05-deployment/01-gitops-fluxcd/03-helm-deployment.md.

tasks.title: Create MariaDB Operator HelmRelease (CC-0008, REQ-002, REQ-003, REQ-008)
tasks.description: Create deploy/flux-system/releases/mariadb-operator.yaml with HelmRelease: apiVersion helm.toolkit.fluxcd.io/v2, metadata.name mariadb-operator, metadata.namespace mariadb-system, spec.interval 30m, chart.spec.chart mariadb-operator, chart.spec.version '>=0.30.0 <1.0.0', sourceRef (kind HelmRepository, name mariadb-operator, namespace flux-system), values.metrics.enabled true, values.webhook.enabled true, install.crds CreateReplace, install.createNamespace true, upgrade.crds CreateReplace, upgrade.remediation.retries 3, dependsOn [{name: cert-manager, namespace: cert-manager}]. Include SPDX header. Exact pattern from architecture/docs/05-deployment/01-gitops-fluxcd/03-helm-deployment.md lines 17-44.

tasks.title: Create External Secrets Operator HelmRelease (CC-0008, REQ-002, REQ-003, REQ-008)
tasks.description: Create deploy/flux-system/releases/external-secrets.yaml with HelmRelease: apiVersion helm.toolkit.fluxcd.io/v2, metadata.name external-secrets, metadata.namespace external-secrets, spec.interval 30m, chart.spec.chart external-secrets, chart.spec.version '>=0.9.0', sourceRef (kind HelmRepository, name external-secrets, namespace flux-system), install.crds CreateReplace, install.createNamespace true, upgrade.crds CreateReplace, upgrade.remediation.retries 3, dependsOn [{name: cert-manager, namespace: cert-manager}]. Include SPDX header.

tasks.title: Create Memcached Operator HelmRelease (CC-0008, REQ-002, REQ-003, REQ-008)
tasks.description: Create deploy/flux-system/releases/memcached-operator.yaml with HelmRelease: apiVersion helm.toolkit.fluxcd.io/v2, metadata.name memcached-operator, metadata.namespace memcached-system, spec.interval 30m, chart.spec.chart memcached-operator, chart.spec.version '>=0.1.0 <1.0.0', sourceRef (kind HelmRepository, name c5c3-charts, namespace flux-system) — NOTE: sourceRef name is c5c3-charts (shared OCI registry, NOT a separate memcached-operator repo), install.crds CreateReplace, install.createNamespace true, upgrade.crds CreateReplace, upgrade.remediation.retries 3, dependsOn [{name: cert-manager, namespace: cert-manager}]. Include SPDX header. Pattern reference: architecture/docs/09-implementation/07-ci-cd-and-packaging.md lines 321-353.

tasks.description: Create deploy/infrastructure/mariadb.yaml with MariaDB CR: apiVersion k8s.mariadb.com/v1alpha1, kind MariaDB, metadata.name openstack-db, metadata.namespace openstack, spec.replicas 3, spec.galera.enabled true, spec.maxScale.enabled true, spec.maxScale.replicas 2, spec.storage.size 100Gi, spec.storage.storageClassName ceph-rbd, spec.metrics.enabled true. Include SPDX header. tasks.description: Create deploy/infrastructure/memcached.yaml with Memcached CR: apiVersion cache.c5c3.io/v1alpha1 (CRITICAL: use cache.c5c3.io NOT memcached.c5c3.io — confirmed by fake CRD at internal/common/testutil/fake_crds/memcached-operator/memcached.yaml and envtest at internal/common/testutil/envtest/integration_test.go:48), kind Memcached, metadata.name openstack-memcached, metadata.namespace openstack, spec.replicas 3, spec.image memcached:1.6, spec.config.maxMemoryMB 1024, spec.config.maxConnections 4096, spec.resources.requests.memory 1Gi, spec.resources.requests.cpu 500m, spec.highAvailability.podAntiAffinity soft, spec.highAvailability.podDisruptionBudget.minAvailable 2, spec.monitoring.enabled true, spec.monitoring.exporter.image prom/memcached-exporter:latest. Include SPDX header. Reference: architecture/docs/03-components/01-control-plane/06-infrastructure-operators.md Memcached section.

tasks.title: Create Kustomization file and validate full manifest build (CC-0008, REQ-007, REQ-008)
tasks.description: Create deploy/flux-system/kustomization.yaml with apiVersion kustomize.config.k8s.io/v1beta1, kind Kustomization, resources listing all 11 files: sources/cert-manager.yaml, sources/mariadb-operator.yaml, sources/external-secrets.yaml, sources/openbao.yaml, sources/c5c3-charts.yaml, releases/cert-manager.yaml, releases/mariadb-operator.yaml, releases/external-secrets.yaml, releases/memcached-operator.yaml, ../infrastructure/mariadb.yaml, ../infrastructure/memcached.yaml. Group entries with comments (# HelmRepository sources, # HelmRelease deployments, # Infrastructure CRs). Include SPDX header. Validate by running kustomize build deploy/flux-system/ and verify all resources appear in output.

tasks.title: Create reference documentation for deploy/ infrastructure manifests (CC-0008, REQ-001 through REQ-008)
tasks.description: Create docs/reference/infrastructure/fluxcd-manifests.md documenting: directory structure (deploy/flux-system/sources/, releases/, deploy/infrastructure/), each HelmRepository with name and URL, each HelmRelease with namespace and chart version, dependency ordering diagram, MariaDB Galera CR field summary, Memcached CR field summary (noting cache.c5c3.io API group), how to deploy (kubectl apply -k deploy/flux-system/), how to add new operators (add source + release + kustomization entry). Reference: architecture/docs/05-deployment/01-gitops-fluxcd/ for context.

test_specifications.story: Platform engineer deploys all infrastructure manifests via single kustomize command
test_specifications.expected: kustomize build deploy/flux-system/ exits with status 0 and outputs valid multi-document YAML

test_specifications.story: Platform engineer deploys all infrastructure manifests via single kustomize command
test_specifications.expected: kustomize build output contains 5 HelmRepository, 4 HelmRelease, 1 ClusterIssuer, 1 MariaDB, 1 Memcached resource

test_specifications.story: Platform engineer creates HelmRepository sources for upstream chart registries
test_specifications.expected: All 5 HelmRepository CRs use apiVersion source.toolkit.fluxcd.io/v1

test_specifications.story: Platform engineer creates HelmRepository sources for upstream chart registries
test_specifications.expected: c5c3-charts HelmRepository has spec.type: oci and url starting with oci://

test_specifications.story: Platform engineer deploys cert-manager as the base infrastructure layer with a self-signed ClusterIssuer
test_specifications.expected: cert-manager HelmRelease has no dependsOn field (base layer)

test_specifications.story: Platform engineer deploys cert-manager as the base infrastructure layer with a self-signed ClusterIssuer
test_specifications.expected: File contains a ClusterIssuer with apiVersion cert-manager.io/v1 and spec.selfSigned

test_specifications.story: Platform engineer deploys infrastructure operators with correct dependency ordering
test_specifications.expected: HelmRelease has install.crds CreateReplace, upgrade.crds CreateReplace, upgrade.remediation.retries 3, interval 30m

test_specifications.story: Platform engineer deploys infrastructure operators with correct dependency ordering
test_specifications.expected: MariaDB, ESO, and Memcached Operator HelmReleases all have dependsOn: [{name: cert-manager, namespace: cert-manager}]

test_specifications.story: Platform engineer deploys infrastructure operators with correct dependency ordering
test_specifications.expected: Memcached Operator HelmRelease sourceRef.name is c5c3-charts (shared OCI registry), NOT memcached-operator

test_specifications.story: Platform engineer provisions a MariaDB Galera cluster for OpenStack services
test_specifications.expected: MariaDB CR has apiVersion k8s.mariadb.com/v1alpha1, 3 replicas, galera enabled, maxScale enabled with 2 replicas, 100Gi ceph-rbd storage, metrics enabled

test_specifications.story: Platform engineer provisions a Memcached cluster for OpenStack session caching
test_specifications.expected: Memcached CR uses apiVersion cache.c5c3.io/v1alpha1 (NOT memcached.c5c3.io/v1alpha1)

test_specifications.story: Platform engineer provisions a Memcached cluster for OpenStack session caching
test_specifications.expected: Memcached CR has 3 replicas, soft anti-affinity, PDB minAvailable 2, monitoring enabled with exporter

test_specifications.story: Platform engineer creates HelmRepository sources for upstream chart registries
test_specifications.expected: Every YAML file under deploy/ starts with SPDX-FileCopyrightText and SPDX-License-Identifier Apache-2.0 comments

test_specifications.story: Platform engineer deploys infrastructure operators with correct dependency ordering
test_specifications.expected: Each HelmRelease sourceRef.name matches a HelmRepository metadata.name in sources/ (cert-manager->cert-manager, mariadb-operator->mariadb-operator, external-secrets->external-secrets, memcached-operator->c5c3-charts)

test_specifications.story: Platform engineer deploys infrastructure operators with correct dependency ordering
test_specifications.expected: ESO HelmRelease metadata.namespace is external-secrets with chart version >=0.9.0

test_specifications.story: Platform engineer deploys all infrastructure manifests via single kustomize command
test_specifications.expected: Adding a new operator (e.g., OpenBao HelmRelease for CC-0009) requires only adding one new source file, one new release file, and two lines in kustomization.yaml resources list

review_criteria: All HelmRepository CRs use apiVersion source.toolkit.fluxcd.io/v1 with correct URLs and namespace flux-system
All HelmRelease CRs use apiVersion helm.toolkit.fluxcd.io/v2 with interval 30m, install.crds CreateReplace, upgrade.crds CreateReplace, upgrade.remediation.retries 3, install.createNamespace true
Dependency chain is correct: cert-manager has no dependsOn; mariadb-operator, external-secrets, and memcached-operator all dependsOn cert-manager
Memcached Operator HelmRelease sourceRef.name is c5c3-charts (shared OCI registry) — NOT a separate memcached-operator repository
Memcached CR uses apiVersion cache.c5c3.io/v1alpha1 — NOT memcached.c5c3.io/v1alpha1
MariaDB Galera CR matches architecture doc: 3 replicas, Galera enabled, MaxScale 2 replicas, 100Gi ceph-rbd, metrics
cert-manager release file includes self-signed ClusterIssuer as second YAML document
kustomize build deploy/flux-system/ succeeds and outputs all 12 resources (5 HelmRepository + 4 HelmRelease + 1 ClusterIssuer + 1 MariaDB + 1 Memcached)
Every HelmRelease sourceRef.name matches a HelmRepository metadata.name defined in sources/
All YAML files include SPDX Apache-2.0 license header (3-line format matching project convention)
implementation_notes:
Infrastructure-only feature: no Go code, no frontend, no API endpoints. All deliverables are static YAML manifests. The fullstack checklist (backend API, frontend hooks, UI components) does not apply.

Architectural decisions:
- Single kustomization.yaml listing all files (no sub-directory kustomization files) for simplicity and explicit resource enumeration.
- ClusterIssuer bundled in same file as cert-manager HelmRelease (multi-document YAML) per feature spec. In FluxCD deployments, HelmRelease installs cert-manager CRDs first; for kubectl apply -k, the ClusterIssuer may need a second apply.
- install.createNamespace: true on all HelmReleases to avoid manual namespace pre-creation.
- No Helm values beyond what architecture docs specify — no password/secret configuration (handled by CC-0009 ESO integration).

Critical gotchas:
1. Memcached API group is cache.c5c3.io (confirmed by fake CRD and envtest), NOT memcached.c5c3.io (used in architecture docs).
2. Memcached Operator chart comes from c5c3-charts OCI repository (shared registry), NOT a separate memcached-operator HelmRepository.
3. The ClusterIssuer is cluster-scoped (no namespace in metadata).

Key files for reference:
- architecture/docs/05-deployment/01-gitops-fluxcd/03-helm-deployment.md: canonical HelmRelease pattern
- architecture/docs/09-implementation/07-ci-cd-and-packaging.md lines 310-353: c5c3-charts OCI pattern
- architecture/docs/03-components/01-control-plane/06-infrastructure-operators.md: MariaDB and Memcached CR specs
- internal/common/testutil/fake_crds/: CRD API group confirmation
